varieties.name: MD-2 (Del Monte Gold Extra Sweet)
varieties.origin: Hawaii, USA (Pineapple Research Institute), 1996 commercial release by Del Monte in Costa Rica
varieties.traits: Cylindrical shape with square shoulders, amber skin when ripe, medium size 1.5-2.0 kg
varieties.flesh_fruit: Bright golden-yellow, very sweet with low acidity (0.4-0.45%), high Brix (17°), small core, non-fibrous, crisp
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high, 12-month maturity, resistant to internal browning

varieties.name: Aus Jubilee
varieties.origin: Australia (Pure Aussie Pineapples breeding program), 1992 onwards, commercial release after extensive trials
varieties.traits: Australian-born and bred variety, sweet and aromatic, low acid
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep golden, sweet with pleasant aroma

varieties.name: Aus Festival
varieties.origin: Australia (Pure Aussie Pineapples breeding program), latest release, early development stage
varieties.traits: Large fruit, sweet coconut aromatics in summer described as 'Pina Colada-like'
varieties.flesh_fruit: Golden-yellow, very sweet, unique coconut aroma
varieties.yield_notes: Good sized fruit, promising commercial traits
varieties.growing_notes: Latest Australian pineapple breeding development. Perfectly suited to humid subtropical climates subtropical climate with distinctive flavor profile. Expected to become consumer favorite.
varieties.availability: Limited availability, Pure Aussie Pineapples (expanding production)

varieties.name: Smooth Cayenne (Kew)
varieties.origin: Venezuela (cultivated by Indigenous peoples), introduced from Cayenne, French Guiana 1820, to Queensland via Kew Gardens, England 1858 and 1881
varieties.traits: Large cylindrical fruit 2.5-3.0 kg, smooth leaves without spines, ovoid medium-sized, turns yellow from base to top when ripening
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale-yellow to yellow, firm, close-textured, juicy, traditional pineapple flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Leading variety for canning, high yields, good fruit shape, 12-16° Brix, 0.5-1.0% acidity

varieties.name: Queen (Common Rough)
varieties.origin: South Africa and Australia (historic cultivation), widely distributed heritage variety
varieties.traits: Small to medium fruit 1-1.5 kg, dwarf compact plant, spiny dark purplish-green leaves, rough skin, conical shape, deeply placed eyes
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep golden-yellow, crisp, less juicy than Cayenne but more aromatic, small core, tender, fine flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Lower than Cayenne, early maturity, produces 2+ suckers, 15-16° Brix, 0.6-0.8% acidity

varieties.name: Ripley Queen (Ripley)
varieties.origin: Queensland, Australia (developed in Queensland), 20th century selection
varieties.traits: Dwarf compact plant, crimson-tinged leaves, reddish-green leaves with brown spots, 3-6 pounds, golden rind when ripe, pale copper when fully mature, irregular bearer
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale-yellow, non-fibrous, very sweet and rich, juicy, sweet-tart balance (13-15° Brix), low fiber
varieties.yield_notes: Takes 22 weeks from flowering to fruit maturity, 1.36-2.7 kg fruit

varieties.name: MacGregor (McGregor, Rough McGregor)
varieties.origin: Queensland, Australia (hybridized from Smooth Cayenne in the 1980s), Australian creation
varieties.traits: Bright yellow rind, 4-7 pounds, square shoulders, larger fruit than typical Queen
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm, sweet, low acidity, incredibly firm with more robust aroma than other Queen varieties

varieties.name: Alexandra
varieties.origin: Queen variety selection, grown in Queensland and other regions
varieties.traits: Queen-type characteristics, moderate size
varieties.flesh_fruit: Golden-yellow, aromatic, sweet

varieties.name: James Queen
varieties.origin: South Africa, also grown in Queensland
varieties.traits: Large size, dark green leaves with reddish spots
varieties.flesh_fruit: Reddish-orange flesh on exterior, yellow inside

varieties.name: Queensland Cayenne
varieties.origin: Queensland, Australia (Queensland Department of Primary Industries), released 1975 after 20 years of breeding and testing
varieties.traits: Dual-purpose cultivar, specifically bred for Queensland conditions
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow, firm, suitable for both fresh market and canning
varieties.growing_notes: Specifically developed for humid subtropical climates/Queensland subtropical climate after two decades of research. Dual-purpose for fresh and processing. Represents Queensland's commitment to pineapple breeding excellence. Well-adapted to local conditions.
varieties.availability: Queensland Department of Agriculture, heritage collections

varieties.name: Hilo
varieties.origin: Hawaii, USA (selected from Smooth Cayenne in Hilo region), 1960
varieties.traits: More compact plant than Smooth Cayenne, 3-5 pounds, softer rind, golden flesh
varieties.flesh_fruit: Golden, juicy, mildly sweet, slightly fibrous
varieties.yield_notes: Smaller, more cylindrical fruit, numerous suckers, no slips
varieties.growing_notes: Cayenne variant suitable for humid subtropical climates. Less export-friendly due to softer rind but excellent tropical treat for local fresh markets. Thrives in zones 10-11 with volcanic soil and sun. Good for Humid subtropical subtropical climate.

varieties.name: Sugarloaf (Kona Sugarloaf, White Sugarloaf, Brazilian White)
varieties.origin: West Africa and Hawaii (also known as Pan de Azúcar in Spanish, Pain de Sucre in French), cultivated since 15th century sugar cone era
varieties.traits: Cylindrical conical shape resembling traditional sugarloaf, 5-6 pounds, green rind remains mostly green when ripe, smooth leaves, softer rind than most varieties
varieties.flesh_fruit: Creamy white to pale yellow, exceedingly sweet with hints of honey, almost no acidity (lowest acid pineapple), non-stringy, floral aroma, can reach 15° Brix, soft edible core (non-woody)

varieties.name: Red Spanish (Black Spanish, Large Key, Cuban)
varieties.origin: Caribbean (possibly Hispaniola), spread across Central and South America by Spanish colonizers, historic variety
varieties.traits: Orange-red to deep red rind, smaller 2-5 pounds, squarish appearance, oval shape, tough skin excellent for shipping, spiny thorny leaves
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale-yellow with hints of color, aromatic, fibrous, acidic and bold flavor, pleasant aroma, light yellow
varieties.yield_notes: 1-2 kg, well-adapted for shipping as fresh fruit, moderate to high

varieties.name: Singapore Red
varieties.origin: Malaysia/Singapore region, Spanish group
varieties.traits: Green leaves with reddish stripe at end, reddish cylindrical fruit, small size
varieties.flesh_fruit: Golden-yellow
varieties.growing_notes: Disease and pest resistance makes it excellent for Humid subtropical humid climate. Great for canning. Subtropical adaptation.
varieties.availability: Specialty Asian variety nurseries, tropical fruit collectors

varieties.name: Kew (Giant Kew, Smooth Cayenne)
varieties.origin: Venezuela (Indians), via Cayenne French Guiana 1820, introduced to India, known as Kew/Sarawak in India
varieties.traits: Large oblong 2-3 kg, slight taper towards crown, broad shallow eyes ideal for canning, yellow when fully ripe
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light yellow, juicy, fiberless, firm close-textured
varieties.yield_notes: High, late-maturing, most popular commercial variety in India, 12-14° Brix, 0.5-1.0% acidity

varieties.name: Queen (Common Rough, Nanas Moris)
varieties.origin: Widely distributed heritage variety, also called Nanas Moris (Malaysia, from Mauritius name), leading cultivar Philippines, Queensland, South Africa
varieties.traits: Dwarf plant, short very spiny dark purplish-green leaves, compact, more cold and disease resistant, 1-2.5 pounds, petite, spiky leaves
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep golden-yellow, tender, sweeter and more aromatic than Smooth Cayenne, small core, crispy, less juicy but higher quality flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Average 2 suckers or more for ratooning, 15-16° Brix, 0.6-0.8% acidity

varieties.name: Jaldhup 🏆
varieties.origin: Jaldhup region, India (named after place of maximum production), traditional Indian variety
varieties.traits: Falls under Queen group, smaller than Queen variety, local type
varieties.flesh_fruit: Well-balanced blend of sweetness with acidity, alcoholic flavor that can be differentiated from other Queen fruits
varieties.growing_notes: INDIGENOUS to India. Named after production region. Cultivated for both table and processing purposes. Perfectly adapted to Tropical monsoon tropical monsoon climate with distinctive local flavor profile. Grown in Meghalaya and tropical monsoon climates. Unique alcoholic flavor note distinguishes it.
varieties.availability: Local tropical monsoon climates markets, Jaldhup region growers, heritage collections

varieties.name: Lakhat 🏆
varieties.origin: Lakhat region, India (named after place of maximum production), traditional Indian variety
varieties.traits: Falls under Queen group, smaller than Queen variety, local type
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet with balanced acidity
varieties.growing_notes: INDIGENOUS to India. Named after production region. Cultivated for both table and processing purposes. Well-adapted to tropical monsoon climates monsoon conditions.
varieties.availability: Local tropical monsoon climates markets, Lakhat region growers, Indian heritage collections

varieties.name: MD-2 (Del Monte Gold Extra Sweet, Dinar)
varieties.origin: Hawaii (Pineapple Research Institute), Costa Rica commercial production 1996, recommended for tropical monsoon climates cultivation
varieties.traits: Cylindrical shape with square shoulders, amber skin, medium 1.5-2.0 kg, international market standard
varieties.flesh_fruit: Bright golden, very sweet, low acidity (0.4-0.45%), high Brix (17°), small core, resistant to internal browning

varieties.name: Abacaxi
varieties.origin: Brazil (abacaxi is Brazilian Portuguese for pineapple), also Florida and Bahamas
varieties.traits: Tall, spiny, 2-11 pounds, bluish-green leaves with red-purple tinge, traditional appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Nearly translucent, white transparent, ultra-sweet, low acid, juicy, one of most delicious types

varieties.name: Pernambuco (Eleuthera)
varieties.origin: Northeastern Brazil (Pernambuco state), also Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, Venezuela
varieties.traits: Vigorous plant, erect growth habit, semi-vigorous, long spiny leaves, oblong cylindrical shape, yellow-orange outside, 2-4 pounds, medium
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale yellow to white, tender, juicy, mildly sweet, sweet melting, excellent flavor
varieties.yield_notes: 1-2 kg, well-adapted to canning and processing, high sugar and acid content
varieties.growing_notes: Cultivation restricted to South American countries and suitable tropical regions. Excellent for eating fresh. Poorly adapted for shipping but perfect for local tropical monsoon climates consumption. Disease resistance trait (used in Maspine hybrid breeding). Thrives in tropical monsoon conditions. Traditional fermented drink aluá made from scraps. Distinctive long spiny leaves. Zones 10-11 with sandy soil and summer blooms. From Brazil's tropical coast.
varieties.availability: Specialty collectors, may be sourced from South American contacts for tropical monsoon climates cultivation trials

varieties.name: Sugarloaf (Pan de Azúcar, Kona)
varieties.origin: West Africa, Hawaii, Puerto Rico, Philippines, South America, Cuba
varieties.traits: Cylindrical shape, tough rind but softer than other varieties, remains green even when ripe, 3-6 pounds
varieties.flesh_fruit: Juicy, creamy texture, white to pale yellow, floral aroma, sweet like honey, exceedingly sweet (up to 15° Brix), almost no acidity, edible non-woody core
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, available year-round with peak mid-late summer

varieties.name: Red Spanish (Española Roja)
varieties.origin: Caribbean, Cuba, Venezuela, Mexico, West Indies, spread by Spanish colonizers
varieties.traits: Orange-red skin, 2-5 pounds, squarish, tough for shipping, spiny leaves
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale yellow, light yellow with high fiber, aromatic, pleasant aroma, acidic bold flavor
varieties.yield_notes: 1-2 kg, well-adapted for shipping and distant markets

varieties.name: Champaka (Champaka-153)
varieties.origin: Cuba (studied in plantations in central Cuba), Spanish group
varieties.traits: Large plant, long dark green spiny sword-like leaves with reddish spots, medium to large fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow to golden
varieties.yield_notes: Encouraging results in Cuban studies for production, disease resistance, and fruit weight
varieties.growing_notes: Currently studied in Cuba with encouraging results. Cuban tropical climate similar to tropical monsoon climates monsoon conditions. Shows good production, disease resistance, and fruit weight. Could be excellent candidate for tropical monsoon climates introduction from Cuban agricultural research.
varieties.availability: Cuban agricultural research stations, potential for tropical monsoon climates cultivation trials

varieties.name: Perola (Pearl)
varieties.origin: Brazil, studied in Cuba
varieties.traits: Brazilian variety with good characteristics
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, good quality
varieties.growing_notes: Shows encouraging results in Cuban central plantation studies for production, disease resistance, and fruit weight. Brazilian origin suggests good tropical monsoon adaptation. Could be suitable for tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.availability: Brazilian/Cuban sources, potential for tropical monsoon climates cultivation

varieties.name: Mordilona
varieties.origin: Northeastern Andes of Colombia and Venezuela, Mordilona group
varieties.traits: Large, irregular, cylindrical, yellow to orange peel
varieties.flesh_fruit: Cream-colored, sweet
varieties.growing_notes: Cultivation restricted to certain South American countries. Genetic contributor to 73-50 hybrid (20% Mordilona). Tropical mountain origin suggests potential for tropical monsoon climates highland areas. Sweet flesh good for fresh consumption.

varieties.name: Panare
varieties.origin: Venezuela (named after Panare Indian tribe who grew it), Bolivar State
varieties.traits: Medium-sized plant, long spiny leaves, bottle-shaped fruit, small 0.45-0.70 kg, small crown, ovate with deep eyes, orange externally
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep yellow, slightly fragrant, little fiber, small core

varieties.name: Maipure
varieties.origin: Venezuela/South America
varieties.traits: Cylindrical, lighter skin, spiky leaves with red spots
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep yellow

varieties.name: Montufar
varieties.origin: Guatemala, Abacaxi group
varieties.traits: Conical shape, greenish rough rind, small to medium leaves with large border spikes
varieties.flesh_fruit: Beautiful bright yellow inside, yellowish-green outside
varieties.yield_notes: Great for juicing - creates tastiest pineapple juice

varieties.name: Brecheche
varieties.origin: Southern Venezuela (limited cultivation)
varieties.traits: Small size, medium olive-colored leaves, cylindrical shape, yellow color, spike-free plant
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very fragrant and juicy, little fiber, small core
varieties.growing_notes: Spike-free advantage for handling and cultivation. Very fragrant with little fiber makes it premium quality. Venezuelan tropical origin suits tropical monsoon climates climate. Small cylinder shape distinctive.
varieties.availability: Venezuelan specialty sources, rare collector variety

varieties.name: Santa Marta (Santa Marta No. 1)
varieties.origin: Colombia (Cayenne strain)
varieties.traits: Subject to cracking of core in hot dry weather
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality when conditions suitable
varieties.growing_notes: Colombian Cayenne strain. Issue with core cracking in hot/dry weather less problematic in Tropical monsoon humid monsoon climate with ample rainfall. Worth testing in tropical monsoon climates conditions.

varieties.name: Cayena Lisa
varieties.origin: Spanish name for Smooth Cayenne, widely grown in Spanish-speaking regions
varieties.traits: Long wide leaves, cylindrical fruit, few spikes at top of leaves
varieties.flesh_fruit: Reddish orange outside, pale-yellow inside

varieties.name: PR-1-67 (Roja Española type)
varieties.origin: Puerto Rico (similar to Roja Española)
varieties.traits: Barrel-shaped, yellow-orange exterior, yellowish-green leaves with reddish tinge
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality
varieties.growing_notes: Puerto Rican selection of Red Spanish type. Puerto Rico's tropical monsoon climate very similar to tropical monsoon climates. Proven tropical adaptation.
varieties.availability: Puerto Rican agricultural sources, Caribbean connections

varieties.name: Spanish Jewel
varieties.origin: Spanish group selection
varieties.traits: Cylindrical shape, rectangular flat deep eyes, wide long dark green leaves, white outside
varieties.flesh_fruit: Orange inside

varieties.name: Sarawak (Nanas Moris - Peninsular Malaysia)
varieties.origin: Malaysia (Peninsular Malaysia), also known in India/Sri Lanka/Thailand as Sarawak/Kew
varieties.traits: Vigorous growing, large about 2.5 kg, egg-shaped fruit on short strong peduncle, broad flat fruitlets/eyes
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow, good quality
varieties.growing_notes: Malaysian tropical climate very similar to tropical monsoon climates monsoon. Already known in India as Kew/Sarawak. Vigorous growth suits Tropical monsoon humid conditions. Does not respond readily to flower induction on peat but excellent on mineral soil.
varieties.availability: Malaysian sources, already present in India as Kew variety, Southeast Asian connections

varieties.name: Maspine (Line 73-50)
varieties.origin: Malaysia (MARDI Station, Pontian, Johore), released May 5, 2005 after 10 years research (1993-2004)
varieties.traits: Manzanah group, medium-sized crown, seldom has stem, aerial and ground shoots, ≥80% flowering response
varieties.flesh_fruit: Golden yellow, high sugar content
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding, productive, red-heart inflorescence 37 days after hormone treatment

varieties.name: Phuket (Queen type)
varieties.origin: Thailand (Phuket region), Queen variety
varieties.traits: Queen characteristics, smaller size
varieties.flesh_fruit: Golden-yellow, aromatic, sweet

varieties.name: Victoria (Queen type)
varieties.origin: Queen variety, various tropical regions
varieties.traits: Queen group characteristics, compact
varieties.flesh_fruit: Golden, sweet, aromatic

varieties.name: Heirloom Varieties: 25+ heritage varieties marked with
